name: Murch
cooked: Since the SegData would cost the same as witness stuffing, but would result in reduced data availability, why would it be attractive to users that want to embed data? Given that most people aren’t interested in improving support for data embedding, I doubt anyone else would consider this worth the effort of a softfork, but if it’s not even attractive for the would-be users, what’s the point?
topic_title: [BIP Draft] Segregated Data: a prunable, script-isolated block region for data carriage
topic_html_title: [BIP Draft] Segregated Data: a prunable, script-isolated block region for data carriage
raw:
Since the SegData would cost the same as witness stuffing, but would result in reduced data availability, why would it be attractive to users that want to embed data?
Given that most people aren't interested in improving support for data embedding, I doubt anyone else would consider this worth the effort of a softfork, but if it's not even attractive for the would-be users, what's the point?
